activeSlice.name: winui-generated-offline-lore-content-pack
activeSlice.goal: make app package candidates include broad offline Lore through a generated short-path lore-pack while preserving Explorer-safe ZIP extraction and hard-payload boundaries
activeSlice.claimBoundary: WinUI/AppCore/package implementation only; no BEA launch, no CDB attach, no byte patch, no Ghidra mutation, no new GitHub release publication, no game payload redistribution, and no online proof.
latestFindings: LoreBrowserService now prefers lore-pack/onslaught-lore.v1.index.json plus onslaught-lore.v1.jsonl when present and falls back to lore-book/BOOK.md otherwise.
The generated Lore pack uses logical lore-pack:// document keys and rewrites included document links to stay in the reader while external/source links open in the browser.

tools/winui_lore_pack_builder.py builds/checks the deterministic pack, includes public Markdown/TXT Lore documents, rewrites unpacked local links to GitHub source/search links, and rejects hard-payload-like source entries.

tools/winui_zip_package_probe.py stages lore-pack/, rejects raw deep lore-book mirror leakage, validates pack schema/hashes/content safety, validates packed local links, and keeps Explorer entry paths under budget.
The latest local package probe generated 943 public-safe offline Markdown/TXT Lore documents and passed extracted launch/Home/Lore/Media smoke tests.
Framework/app image assets under app/ are allowed by the package probe; texture/media-like images outside known framework paths remain blocked.

lorePackaging.currentRelease: v1.0.6 is still the latest published GitHub app ZIP; it predates the generated lore-pack implementation.
lorePackaging.currentSource: Package candidates now stage lore-book/BOOK.md and Start-Here.md as short entry files plus generated lore-pack/onslaught-lore.v1.index.json and onslaught-lore.v1.jsonl with 943 public-safe Markdown/TXT Lore documents.
lorePackaging.decision: Do not raw-copy the full deep lore-book tree into the app ZIP; use the generated short-path content pack to avoid Explorer path-too-long extraction failures.
